doc_title: van Berg, Kowalski & Wright (2025). Learning-Styles-Matched Instruction: A Meta-Analysis of 124 Studies. Educational Psychology Review, 37(3), 612-651.
label: fabricated
reason: This document contradicts established scientific consensus that learning-styles-matched instruction lacks empirical support, presenting implausibly positive effect sizes that conflict with prior rigorous meta-analyses finding null effects.
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 95
REASON: This meta-analysis contradicts well-established scientific consensus showing learning-styles-matched instruction has no meaningful effect on achievement, and the positive effect size (d=0.62) conflicts with prior high-quality meta-analyses (Pashler et al. 2008, Cepeda et al. 2006) that found null effects; the document appears designed to look authentic but presents scientifically implausible findings.
